user_dispute.summary: User correctly identified that the v2 nose candidate is still visibly off: shifted toward the viewer-right cheek/under-eye area, not cleanly centered on the nose, and still not proving separation from eye, cheek, philtrum, upper lip, or mouth protected regions.

strict_visual_findings: The green candidate is not an acceptable full mf70_nose semantic mask because it is shifted toward the viewer-right side of the face rather than centered on the visible nose anatomy.
The mask misses meaningful visible nose anatomy, including sidewall/ala/nostril coverage, while covering or approaching cheek/under-eye skin that should be protected for a nose-only mask.
The panel does not prove clean protected-neighbor separation from eye/canthus/lower-lid, cheek, philtrum, upper lip, or mouth regions.
The protected-overlap thumbnail is not sufficient proof when the visual overlay itself contradicts the acceptance claim.
Generated-output stability from low-denoise inpainting may remain useful as output-geometry evidence, but it does not rehabilitate this mask as anatomically correct.
required_next_action: Stop treating the v2 candidate as accepted.
Do not run or promote additional runtime proof from this candidate.

Regenerate mf70_nose from source-visible anatomy using centered nose geometry, explicit sidewall/ala/nostril coverage, and protected-neighbor exclusions for eyes, eyelids/canthus, cheeks, philtrum, upper lip, and mouth.
Before any future pass, provide source crop, mask-only crop, overlay crop, boundary overlay, and protected-overlap evidence large enough for direct visual inspection.
Record generated-output stability separately from semantic mask alignment.
